step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the selection of option D for indicators of fetal lung maturity. Additionally, it’s important to note that blue cells detected through the Nile Blue Staining test specifically signify the presence of immature surfactant-producing type II pneumocytes, directly opposing mature lung function. This makes them an excellent marker distinguishing between maturation states without any ambiguity compared to other options like increased lecithin/sphingomyelin ratios or positive results from tests such as those detecting phosphatidylglycerol levels, all of which correlate positively with advancing lung development stages.
